New arrangements will be made
by the office of the postal service
as to the mail delivery between
Spokane and Wallace, Idaho. Be-
cause of the new schedule of the
o. r. & N, Tekoa will be taken Off.


The president of the Northwest
ern corporation. controlling a num-
Der of lighting and irrigation plants,
Isaac w. Anderson, will arrive in
Spokane next week. After an in
Mr. Anderson will leave for Lewis
ton.


A suit was brought against the
ThompsOn-Starr Co., who have
the contract for building the new
steel Is story Old National bank
building, by Walter II. Emmerson
for $500 for injuries said to have
been received by negligence on the
part of the companys foreman in
allowing a bucket of stone to strike
him.


} It is given out in reports of the
United States reclamation service
that many emigrants are coming
from the Canadian northwest to set
tie in the Inland Empire.


More than 200 fruit packers are
wanted all along the Snake river
this season, and good wages are Of
fered for the next 80 days.


Susie, the aged Indian squaw Iiv-
ing in an Indian camp west of SpO-
kane, fell from horse she was rid
ing five weeks ago and has been
unable to move about since. Yes
terday Dr.. j. Glen HARRISON and
Miss M. L. Hornburg visited the
